steps.title: Find the shared viewpoint
steps.content: The question asks what both authors would most likely agree with. Text 1 says collaboration between tribal groups and government agencies has built trust and will likely improve joint conservation projects. Text 2 says incorporating traditional ecological knowledge builds positive relationships and tends to lead to better conservation outcomes.

steps.title: Eliminate unsupported effects
steps.content:
• Choice A - 'A collaborative approach to conservation is likely to help reduce the predicted temperature increases in the Great Lakes region.': Neither text claims that collaboration can reduce temperature increases. Both texts discuss collaboration as a way to improve conservation work.
  • Choice B - 'In the Great Lakes region, conservation efforts focused on the northern wild rice plant are likely to be more effective than those focused on the American crow.': Text 1 compares climate vulnerability for wild rice and crows, but neither text compares the effectiveness of conservation efforts focused on those species.

steps.title: Match both texts
steps.content:
• Choice D - 'In the Great Lakes region, it is more important to focus conservation efforts on the northern wild rice plant than it is on the American crow.': Neither author ranks those species by conservation priority. Text 1 discusses likely climate effects, not relative importance.
  • Choice C - 'Natural resources conservation projects are likely to benefit from collaboration between tribal and government groups.': This matches Text 1's claim that collaboration can improve joint projects and Text 2's claim that the approach can produce better outcomes. The answer is C.
